Products.headline: We live our own products.
Products.subhead: It's what lets us build yours with the rigor of someone who knows what it costs to keep a product alive.

ProductsIndex.headline: Four verticals. Designed, launched, maintained.
ProductsIndex.subhead: If one of these markets is yours, the tool may already exist. Otherwise, we'll build one with you.

ProductDetail.interestBody: The product isn't open to the public yet, but you can join the priority testers list. Promise: we get back to you within 48h.
ProductDetail.formSuccess: Got it. We'll come back to you within 48h.

ProductCreneau.name: Créneau
ProductCreneau.tagline: The marketplace digitizing Dakar driving schools.
ProductCreneau.description: A single platform for driving schools: student management, instructors, schedules, payments — and a public marketplace that automatically brings in new signups.
ProductCreneau.problem: The typical driving school keeps books in a notebook, students on WhatsApp, schedules in the manager's head. No visibility, lots of leaks, zero digital acquisition.
ProductCreneau.solution: Centralized dashboard for the manager, autonomous student space, dedicated marketplace exposing each driving school and converting prospects into direct signups.
ProductCreneau.feature1: Centralized management of students, instructors, schedules, payments
ProductCreneau.feature2: Public marketplace exposing each registered school
ProductCreneau.feature3: Direct signup link from each school's ad
ProductCreneau.feature4: Audience generated by TerangaDev side (SEO + acquisition)

ProductNurapic.name: Nurapic
ProductNurapic.tagline: Your event photos, found with a selfie.
ProductNurapic.description: The photographer uploads their thousands of event photos. Guests scan their face with their phone and AI instantly returns photos featuring them — which they can buy online.
ProductNurapic.problem: An event photographer takes 500 to 2,000 photos per night. To sell them, they have to find each guest one by one. Result: most photos are never sold.
ProductNurapic.solution: AI face recognition + public link shared by the photographer + unified guest interface. Guests find themselves and buy their photos directly online.
ProductNurapic.feature1: Unlimited upload on the photographer side
ProductNurapic.feature3: Guest interface: find all your photos across every event
ProductNurapic.feature4: Online payment · Wave, Orange Money, card

ProductSama.name: Sama Reservation
ProductSama.tagline: The hair, massage and beauty salon without the ringing phone.
ProductSama.description: A platform for salons (hair, massage, aesthetics) that digitizes appointment booking, staff management, and the calendar — while clients book online in a few clicks.
ProductSama.problem: The typical salon spends half its day answering the phone for appointments. Double bookings are frequent, cancellations unmanaged, and empty slots impossible to fill.
ProductSama.solution: Centralized agenda by staff member, online booking 24/7, automated reminders, and a dashboard to optimize occupancy.
ProductSama.feature1: 24/7 online booking by service and staff

ProductTicket.name: Ticket-Events
ProductTicket.tagline: Web ticketing for African concerts, conferences and trade shows.
ProductTicket.description: An event ticketing platform built for the African market: Wave / Orange Money / card payment, QR tickets, mobile access control, and self-service organizer management.
ProductTicket.problem: Event organizers in Senegal juggle Eventbrite (poorly suited to local payments), Excel sheets and WhatsApp. Access control is manual, fraud is common.
ProductTicket.solution: Self-service platform where the organizer creates their event in 10 minutes, sells tickets through all African payment channels, and scans QR codes at the entrance with their phone.
ProductTicket.feature2: Payment via Wave, Orange Money, bank card
ProductTicket.feature3: Secure QR tickets + mobile scanner for access control

WhyUs.headline: We've coded, launched and maintained 4 SaaS under our own brand.
WhyUs.subhead: We know what it costs to keep a product alive — that's why we walk yours all the way.
WhyUs.pillar1Body: We live what we build. Not an agency that ships and vanishes.
WhyUs.pillar2Body: Your code, our team in Dakar, your hosting choice. Zero vendor lock-in.
WhyUs.pillar3Body: Ideation, dev, marketing, training, maintenance — one single point of contact.
WhyUs.pillar4Body: Hydrautech in <2 months. Ndougalma 2 months early. The rule, not the exception.

Work.headline: Three projects that say what we do.
Work.case1Metric: Shipped in <2 months · 10+ staff trained · 200+ items tracked

WorkIndex.headline: The projects we shipped that are still running.
WorkIndex.subhead: We only show projects we know end to end — from brief to maintenance. If you're looking for a generic demo, this isn't it.

CaseDetail.nextDefault: Maintenance contract, quarterly evolutions and ongoing team training — we stay on deck.
CaseDetail.ctaBody: If your problem looks like this one, we can probably ship yours within comparable timelines.

CaseHydrautech.name: Hydrautech
CaseHydrautech.scope: Marketing site · management system · team training · maintenance
CaseHydrautech.tagline: We replaced the stock notebook with a platform. 200+ items tracked in under 2 months.
CaseHydrautech.description: Hydrautech is a Dakar-based industrial SME specialized in supplying and maintaining hydraulic equipment for Senegalese industry. Over 10 employees, dozens of active sites, and — before us — zero digital visibility on stock or operations.
CaseHydrautech.brief: Hydrautech's manager came to us with a simple goal: get out of paper-based management. He wanted a professional marketing site for the brand and a management system that would finally give him visibility on equipment, stock and teams — without imposing a brutal change on staff who weren't computer-savvy.
CaseHydrautech.challenge: Everything lived on paper or in the manager's head: equipment in/out tracked in a notebook, breakdowns reported by phone, invoices written by hand. Each error cost hours of searching, and impossible to answer a client in real time on equipment availability. The risk of silent losses was huge.
CaseHydrautech.approach: We scoped the project in two phases: first a modern marketing site to establish the brand, then a custom back-office to digitize management. The system had to be readable by employees who had never used management software — so simplified interface, no jargon, and 2 days of on-site training per role (manager, stock keeper, technician).
CaseHydrautech.phase1Body: One week on site to understand real flows — how each item enters, exits, returns, gets repaired. We mapped 4 distinct roles and 12 critical use cases.
CaseHydrautech.phase2Body: Visual identity reworked, responsive site in French, hydraulic catalog product sheets, quote form, local SEO. Shipped in 3 weeks.
CaseHydrautech.phase3Body: Filament back-office on top of Laravel + PostgreSQL. Equipment inventory with physical QR codes, intervention tracking, low stock alerts, accounting exports.
CaseHydrautech.phase4Body: 2 days of on-site training for the 10+ employees. Annual maintenance contract: security updates, quarterly evolutions, N1 and N2 support.
CaseHydrautech.stack: Laravel 12 · PostgreSQL · Filament · Docker · OVH France · Datamatrix QR codes

CaseNdougalma.name: Ndougalma
CaseNdougalma.scope: Ideation · business model · platform · digital marketing · maintenance
CaseNdougalma.tagline: An idea for a grocery shopping app. Live 2 months ahead of the original plan.
CaseNdougalma.description: A Senegalese entrepreneur came to us with an idea: let Senegalese people do their grocery shopping online, prioritizing local merchants. No existing platform, no marketing strategy, no formalized business model. Everything to build.
CaseNdougalma.brief: The brief was a single sentence: "I want to launch an app that would allow Senegalese to do their grocery shopping online." The client knew their market but had neither product experience nor technical expertise to execute. They were looking for a partner to take charge of the entire project — from idea to first euro earned.
CaseNdougalma.challenge: An idea isn't a product. We needed to validate the business model (average basket, margins, delivery logistics), define the precise target, choose an MVP that generates revenue without costing a fortune to maintain, and build marketing traction before public launch.
CaseNdougalma.approach: We laid out a 4-phase roadmap over 5 months — which we ultimately compressed to 3 months thanks to client availability and clear scoping from day one. Modern stack (Next.js + local payment Wave + Stripe for diaspora), light backend to iterate fast, and a digital marketing track running in parallel with technical build so go-live wasn't an empty page.
CaseNdougalma.phase1Body: Three 2-hour workshops on average basket, initial targeting, delivery logistics, and product margin. Output: a testable BM and a 6-month roadmap.
CaseNdougalma.phase2Body: Responsive Next.js web app with catalog, cart, checkout, user accounts, Wave + Orange Money + card payment. Deliberately light to iterate fast.
CaseNdougalma.phase3Body: Visual identity, social media launch, paid teasing campaign before go-live, partnerships with Dakar grocery micro-influencers.
CaseNdougalma.phase4Body: Production on OVH France, client training on admin, quarterly maintenance and evolution contract, performance monitoring.
CaseNdougalma.stack: Next.js 15 · PostgreSQL · Stripe · Wave Money · Orange Money · OVH France · Sentry

ServicesIndex.headline: Seven services. No magic packages, no vague promises.
ServicesIndex.subhead: We'd rather show ranges than hide prices in a sales tunnel. If it sounds like too much, we're probably not a fit anyway.

ServiceDetail.ctaBody: Let's talk for 30 minutes to scope and time it.
ServiceDetail.priceQuoteText: Fixed quote after scoping. Final price depends on your IT footprint, user count or project ambition.
ServiceDetail.priceCustomText: No per-piece pricing. We define a quarterly or annual envelope tailored to your goals together.
ServiceDetail.priceRangeNote: Final price depends on complexity, functional scope and ongoing maintenance contract length. We quote precisely after scoping.

ServicePlatforms.name: Management platforms
ServicePlatforms.tagline: When your business lives on paper or in the manager's head, we build the system that gives you back the wheel.
ServicePlatforms.description: For SMEs, mid-market companies and multi-site organizations that have outgrown spreadsheets and want a tailored business tool — not a generic SaaS that only fits 60% of their needs.
ServicePlatforms.deliverable1: Centralized dashboard with the metrics that matter to you
ServicePlatforms.deliverable2: Custom modules: stock, HR, accounting, scheduling, billing — based on your activity
ServicePlatforms.deliverable3: Tiered roles & permissions (manager, supervisor, operator, read-only)
ServicePlatforms.deliverable4: Accounting exports, third-party API integrations, automatic alerts
ServicePlatforms.deliverable6: Annual maintenance contract: security updates, quarterly evolutions, N1+N2 support
ServicePlatforms.phase1Body: We spend a week with you to map your real flows — not what the org chart says.
ServicePlatforms.phase2Body: We deliver a prototype in 2-3 weeks to validate the architecture with your teams before coding the full system.
ServicePlatforms.phase3Body: Short-iteration development with weekly demos. You test as we go, not at the end.
ServicePlatforms.phase4Body: On-site training for each role. Maintenance contract setup to ensure continuity over time.

ServiceProject.name: A-Z project support
ServiceProject.tagline: You have an idea. We ship a platform in production with your first euro earned.
ServiceProject.description: For founders with the business vision but not the technical background. We take the project end to end: business model, dev, marketing, training, maintenance.
ServiceProject.deliverable1: Business model scoping and 6-12 month roadmap
ServiceProject.deliverable3: African payment integrations: Wave, Orange Money, card
ServiceProject.deliverable4: Digital marketing strategy + go-live coordinated launch
ServiceProject.phase1Body: BM workshops, targeting, pricing, logistics. Output: a testable business model and a 6-month roadmap.
ServiceProject.phase2Body: We code the MVP focusing on functions that generate revenue from the first month.
ServiceProject.phase3Body: Visual identity, social media, paid teasing campaign, partnerships with local influencers.
ServiceProject.phase4Body: Production launch, client training, KPI monitoring, quarterly optimizations.

ServiceBrand.name: Brand identity + marketing site
ServiceBrand.tagline: A recognizable brand and a converting site. Not a copy-pasted Wix template.
ServiceBrand.description: For companies and professionals who want to look serious and stop sending prospects to a Facebook page that smells amateur.
ServiceBrand.deliverable1: Logo + brand guidelines + palette + typography + variations
ServiceBrand.deliverable2: Responsive marketing site in French (EN option)
ServiceBrand.deliverable3: Services / work / contact pages + qualifying form
ServiceBrand.deliverable4: Basic SEO: meta tags, sitemap, schema.org, page speed
ServiceBrand.deliverable5: Deployment on your hosting or ours
ServiceBrand.phase1Body: 30 minutes to clarify your positioning, audience, inspirations and anti-inspirations.
ServiceBrand.phase2Body: We present 2 visual directions, you pick. We refine in 1-2 iterations max.
ServiceBrand.phase3Body: Responsive mockups, validation, dev, content integration.
ServiceBrand.phase4Body: Hosting, DNS, SSL, monitoring, content update training.

ServiceAuditIT.name: IT audit
ServiceAuditIT.tagline: Full map of your IT system. Spot risks before they cost you.
ServiceAuditIT.description: For SMEs that have accumulated hardware and software over time without inventory or global visibility. We document, analyze and deliver an action plan.
ServiceAuditIT.deliverable1: Network map (workstations, servers, switches, remote access)
ServiceAuditIT.phase1Body: Interviews, network scan, access to critical workstations, review of existing documentation.
ServiceAuditIT.phase2Body: Cross-checking findings: what's obsolete, vulnerable, misconfigured, or redundant.
ServiceAuditIT.phase3Body: Actionable document with priorities, cost estimates and 6-month plan.
ServiceAuditIT.phase4Body: Presentation meeting with your technical team and decision-makers.

ServiceAuditSec.name: Security audit
ServiceAuditSec.tagline: We simulate the attacker. You see what they see, we tell you how to close the door.
ServiceAuditSec.description: For companies, NGOs or institutions handling sensitive data who want an honest snapshot of their attack exposure.
ServiceAuditSec.deliverable1: Penetration tests (black-box or grey-box per scope)
ServiceAuditSec.deliverable3: Security code review on critical applications
ServiceAuditSec.phase1Body: Clear definition of scope, in-scope assets, rules of engagement, and legal authorizations.
ServiceAuditSec.phase2Body: Recon, exploitation, privilege escalation, persistence — within the defined scope.
ServiceAuditSec.phase3Body: Findings compilation, CVSS scoring, technical and organizational recommendations.
ServiceAuditSec.phase4Body: Executive + technical readout. Optional follow-through on the remediation phase.

ServiceNetwork.name: Networks & VoIP
ServiceNetwork.tagline: We wire, we configure, we document. Your network becomes an asset, not a pain.
ServiceNetwork.description: For companies reorganizing, moving, or growing who want a professional network designed to last 5+ years.
ServiceNetwork.deliverable2: Network design: topology, VLAN, segmentation, IP plan
ServiceNetwork.deliverable3: Hardware supply & install (switches, routers, access points)
ServiceNetwork.deliverable4: VoIP: PBX, handsets, mobile integration, IVR
ServiceNetwork.phase1Body: Site visit, measurements, business needs assessment, 3-year growth anticipation.
ServiceNetwork.phase2Body: Architecture plan, hardware choices, flow simulation. Validation before purchases.
ServiceNetwork.phase4Body: Plans, procedures, admin training. Optional ongoing support contract.

ServiceMarketing.name: Digital marketing
ServiceMarketing.tagline: We bring the audience that pays you. Not LinkedIn vanity.
ServiceMarketing.description: For B2B and B2C companies that want qualified traffic, hot leads and measurable ROI — not a pretty homepage with zero conversion.
ServiceMarketing.deliverable1: Content strategy aligned with your value proposition
ServiceMarketing.deliverable3: Paid campaigns: Meta Ads, Google Ads, LinkedIn Ads per target
ServiceMarketing.deliverable5: Quarterly reporting with KPIs: traffic, leads, cost per acquisition
ServiceMarketing.phase1Body: Existing audit, persona targeting, channel selection, objectives and KPIs.
ServiceMarketing.phase2Body: Content creation, copy, visuals, site optimization.
ServiceMarketing.phase3Body: Campaign launch, social media animation, partnerships.
ServiceMarketing.phase4Body: A/B testing, targeting adjustments, scaling what works.

Contact.headline: Got a project? Let's talk for 30 minutes.
Contact.subhead: Three channels at equal weight. Pick the one that fits you.
Contact.channel2Body: A slot in our calendar. 30 min, qualifying.
Contact.channel3Body: For voice-first folks. We move fast.

Studio.headline: We are six, in Dakar.
Studio.subhead: Not a freelancer who outsources. Not an agency stacking juniors. A stable team since 2022 building its own products and our clients'.
Studio.historyBody: TerangaDev started in 2022 with a simple idea: prove that a Dakar-based studio can ship products under its own brand, not just sell dev hours. Four years later, we run 4 SaaS in production, 6 clients on active maintenance, and a team of six people sharing the same obsession: keep alive the products we build.
Studio.manifestoSubhead: Four principles guiding every technical decision we make. Not a slogan — an operational frame.
Studio.manifesto1Body: TypeScript schemas, source code in your repo. You can migrate elsewhere tomorrow — we just built it together so you don't want to.
Studio.manifesto2Body: Technical decisions made here. No opaque outsourcing to other countries. No timezone gap blocking arbitration.
Studio.manifesto3Body: OVH France by default. Oracle Senegal available. GCP, AWS, your own VPS — you pick, we deploy. No forced cloud lock-in.
Studio.manifesto4Body: Signed payment plan, announced milestones. Hydrautech in <2 months, Ndougalma 2 months early. The rule, not the exception.
Studio.teamSubhead: Six people. Mastered stack, not stacked.

TeamArona.name: Arona Momo
TeamArona.body: The studio's "Serigne bi". Product architect, fullstack Laravel for 8 years. Business vision + hands in the code.

TeamBabacar.name: Babacar Diop
TeamBabacar.body: Production stack guardrail. If something has been running 2 years without alerts, he's been through it.

TeamMouhamed.name: Mouhamed
TeamMouhamed.body: Art direction and frontend. You're reading this site thanks to him.

TeamRane.name: Rane
TeamRane.body: Filament on top of Laravel — his signature. Turns a spec into a back-office in 3 weeks.

TeamIbnou.name: Ibnou
TeamIbnou.body: React Native + web. Nurapic's mobile interface is his work.

TeamYabaye.name: Yabaye
TeamYabaye.body: Docker, Ansible, OVH. If your infrastructure doesn't crash at 3am, thank his crons.

Rejoindre.headline: We hire when there's a role that deserves one more human.
Rejoindre.subhead: No permanent openings right now. But we always read spontaneous applications from people who genuinely love their craft — and have a vision for what TerangaDev could become with them.
Rejoindre.howWeWork1Body: Studio at Point E 2-3 days/week. The rest from your place. No surveillance, just outcomes.
Rejoindre.howWeWork2Body: Notion + Linear + Slack. Short meetings, long writing. We respect everyone's focus.
Rejoindre.howWeWork3Body: You write, deploy and maintain your code. No old-school dev/ops split.
Rejoindre.whatWeLookFor1: Real mastery of at least one mainstream stack (Laravel, Next.js, React Native, etc.)
Rejoindre.whatWeLookFor2: Desire to work on products kept in production, not project churn
Rejoindre.whatWeLookFor3: Enough autonomy to ship a feature end-to-end
Rejoindre.whatWeLookFor4: Respect for deadlines and your word — non-negotiable
Rejoindre.spontaneousBody: Email us at hello@terangadev.com with a CV, a portfolio (GitHub / Dribbble / Behance), and 3 lines on what you really want. No cover letter — a 30-minute call beats it.

ContactPage.headline: Got a project? Let's talk for 30 minutes.
ContactPage.subhead: The form qualifies in a few fields. If you prefer voice, WhatsApp is right next to it. If you'd rather book directly, Calendly is too.
ContactPage.formTimelinePlaceholder: When do you want to start?
ContactPage.formMessage: Describe your project in a few lines
ContactPage.formSuccess: Got it. We'll come back to you within 24h.
ContactPage.formError: Something went wrong. Try again or email us at contact@terangadev.com.
ContactPage.calendlyBody: 30 minutes to scope together. Pick a slot in our calendar.
ContactPage.whatsappBody: For voice-first folks. We move fast and take meetings if there's a fit.

NotesIndex.headline: We write what we learn. No SEO blog.
NotesIndex.subhead: Three angles: technical choices in production, product retros, points of view on African digital sovereignty. Brief reads, assumed opinions.
NotesIndex.comingSoonBody: Editorial pipeline starts in the coming weeks. Three angles we're preparing:
NotesIndex.preview1Title: Why we picked Filament instead of writing a custom back-office
NotesIndex.preview1Body: The Hydrautech lesson: 70% of back-office work is CRUD. Filament does that 70% in 2 weeks. The remaining 30%, we code.
NotesIndex.preview2Title: Three months of Nurapic in production: what we didn't see coming
NotesIndex.preview2Body: Face recognition + casual guests + Dakar 3G connection = an optimization pipeline we hadn't anticipated.
NotesIndex.preview3Title: Your data on OVH France beats AWS US — here's why
NotesIndex.preview3Body: Narrative consistency, GDPR, West Africa latency, vendor lock-in. An honest comparison for African SMEs.
NotesIndex.ctaHeadline: Want to be notified for the first article?
NotesIndex.ctaBody: Email us at hello@terangadev.com with "notes" as subject. We'll add you to the list — no spam, no automated newsletter.

NoteDetail.ctaBody: If what's written here resonates with a problem you have, let's talk.

NotFound.headline: This page doesn't exist. Or not anymore.
NotFound.subhead: Either the URL has a typo, or the page moved. No drama — we'll get you where you want to go.
